title: calendula lusitanica var. microcephala
intro: Calendula lusitanica var. microcephala, also known as dwarf pot marigold, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its small, bright yellow-orange flowers and edible leaves. The medicinal properties of Calendula lusitanica var. microcephala include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and menstrual disorders. Calendula lusitanica var. microcephala can be prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or poultice, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
